revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[ja:愛宕塚古墳 (壬生町)]]
{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.432694|N|139.809972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Atagozuka Kofun | native_name =愛宕塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Atagozuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Atago Jinja located on Atagozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Atagozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|57.7|N|139|48|35.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|qid=Q1354910|lt=Mibu, Tochigi|1=Mibu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} {{nihongo|'''Atagozuka Kofun'''|愛宕塚古墳 ||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|qid=Q1354910|lt=Mibu|1=Mibu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|qid=Q1202834|lt=Shimotsuga District|1=Shimotsuga District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/473 |title=愛宕塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Atagozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the west bank of the Kurokawa River, in the northeast of the town center of Mibu Town, and is part of a group of tumuli, which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)|Ushizuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers and has a total length of 77 meters for the bottom tier and 53 meters for the upper tier. This construction is typical of ''kofun'' in this region and period for the rectangular portion to be larger and taller than the circular portion. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of over twenty meters, portions of which survive.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> ;Overall length: 77 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 23 meter diameter x 5.5 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 35 meters wide x 6 meter high, 2-tier The tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but from the design of the tumulus and from fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' (including one of a man in armor) and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the mid-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the Kofun period when the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke area|1=Shimotsuke Province}} was ruled by independent local kings and the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, when the centralized {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state|1=Yamato Kingship}} dominated. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|en|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|qid=Q11361381|lt=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|1=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji}} indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The top of the tumulus is occupied by a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} (the Atago Shrine) from which the ''kofun'' gets its name. This shrine was built in 1694 by Matsudaira Terusada, ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Mibu Domain|en|Mibu Domain|qid=Q3034496|lt=Mibu Domain|1=Mibu Domain}}, as it was located in the ''Kimon'', or "unlucky direction" from {{ill|Mibu Castle|en|Mibu Castle|qid=Q11429498|lt=Mibu Castle|1=Mibu Castle}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The Atagoyama Kofun is about a 15 minute walk from {{ill|Mibu Station|en|Mibu Station|qid=Q6827788|lt=Mibu Station|1=Mibu Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] {{ill|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|en|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|qid=Q3449834|lt=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|1=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan|qq=}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== * [https://www.tochigiji.or.jp/spot/5058/ Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[http://www.mibu.ed.jp/d_map/link/0004/P0004.html Mibu Department of Education]
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)]]
